channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Editorial: Musk bid for OpenAI exposes hypocrisy of business model
item.description: OpenAI masquerades as a nonprofit despite being founded on the theft of hard work by artists, academics and journalists.
item.dc:creator: Mercury News & East Bay Times editorial, The Mercury News

item.title: Elon Musk’s war on USAID is a war on reality
item.description: On January 29, President Donald Trump celebrated the latest victory in unelected billionaire Elon Musk’s crusade against “inefficiency” in the government: stopping $50 million from, according to Trump, “being sent to Gaza to buy condoms for Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Worrying YouTube security flaw exposed billions of user emails
item.description: Experts find a simple flaw could have exposed any email attached to a YouTube account until recently.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Moss Landing battery fire: Residents sue Vistra, others for damages
item.description: A group of Monterey County residents filed a lawsuit in Alameda County against several companies for damages following the large fire at the Moss Landing Battery Plant on Jan. 16.
item.dc:creator: Kyarra Harris, The Mercury News

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE team may need a crash course in COBOL
item.description: Young software engineers from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) continue to infiltrate U.S. government agencies with the stated goal of eliminating what the Trump White House deems wasteful (or “woke”) spending.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia forges healthcare partnerships to advance AI-driven genomics, drug discovery and more
item.description: Healthcare has the most significant upside, particularly in patient care, among all the industries applicable to AI. The healthcare industry creates massive amounts of data, but much of it lives in silos.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bay Area pioneer in conversational AI looks to the future
item.description: With improving technology and more applications for the use of artificial intelligence, AI could contribute $20 trillion to the global economy by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Severance is officially Apple TV Plus' biggest hit for years – but it’s still miles behind Netflix’s Squid Game
item.description: Apple's streaming service has a lot to celebrate after the latest Neilson viewing stats, with Severance posting the best numbers since a certain Ted Lasso.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How AI is empowering tech leaders
item.description: When it comes to IT procurement, “time is money” is an understatement. For many organizations, the procurement process eats up six to nine months — an expensive exercise in a time of digital transformation, when both tech agility and organizational leadership define business success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Your new Android notification superpower
item.description: It may seem like a paradox, but notifications are both the best and the worst part of owning an Android device. On the one hand, notifications let us stay on top of important incoming info — be it a critical Slack message, a personal family text, or an email from a high-priority client or colleague.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 revealing stats about career challenges Black IT pros face
item.description: The Trump administration spent its first two weeks in office dismantling a raft of policies and agencies, including DEI programs from government and federal jobs, as well as removing data and reports on DEI topics from government websites.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CaixaBank impulsa el Plan Cosmos con una inyección de 5.000 millones para su transformación digital
item.description: CaixaBank pisa el acelerador de su plan de transformación digital contemplado en la hoja de ruta estratégica de la compañía definida para el periodo 2025-2027. Así lo ha avanzado Expansión.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: JetBrains’ Ktor adds CLI for simpler project creation
item.description: JetBrains’ Ktor 3.1.0, an update to the Kotlin-based framework for asynchronous server-side and client-side applications, is now available, featuring a command-line tool for simpler creation of projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum threatened to sue Google Thursday over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Instagram’s AI bots are often sexually suggestive—and sometimes underage
item.description: When Meta launched its “AI Studio” feature for over two billion Instagram users in July 2024, the company promised a tool that would give anyone the ability to create their own AI characters “to make you laugh, generate memes, give travel advice, and so much more.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: State says $400 million armored EV contract on hold
item.description: The State Department is placing a $400 million contract to build armored electric vehicles (EVs) on hold, a spokesperson told The Hill on Thursday. Tech billionaire Elon Musk’s Tesla appears to have been a likely recipient of the contract, which originated from the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: BBC: Chatbots distort the facts about news
item.description: It’s already known that today’s generative AI (genAI) tools often have trouble with basic facts. Now, it’s clear they don’t well with current events either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to delete multiple text messages on a Mac and iPad
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains the steps to declutter text messages on a Mac or iPad. Apple offers ways to do this more efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rust memory management explained
item.description: The Rust programming language shares many concepts with other languages intended for systems programming. For instance, Rust distinguishes between memory allocated from the stack and memory allocated from the heap.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order
item.description: Ilona Cohen, the chief legal and policy officer at HackerOne, explains why President Joe Biden’s new cyber executive order should be a priority. The post Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KREX 5
